observations.recommendation:
1.2 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
a. Instruct the Inventory Committee to follow the guidelines and
procedures as provided for in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020 in the conduct of physical count of PPE, recognition of
those found at station and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE
items for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts of the Municipality;
b. Ensure compliance with the prescribed procedures for the proper
disposition of unserviceable properties;
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to: (1) maintain PPELCs to support the
recorded PPE items and facilitate the reconciliation between the
accounting and property records, (2) reclassify or derecognize all tangible
items below the capitalization threshold from the PPE account, and (3)
submit a complete and accurate lapsing schedule to substantiate the
balances of the Accumulated Depreciation and Depreciation Expense
accounts; and
d. Require the Municipal Assessor to secure the necessary documents to
support the ownership claims of the Municipality over the purchased land
costing ₱10,000,000.00.

2.7 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to
provide the corresponding depreciation of the Infrastructure Assets to
reflect the correct carrying amount of the PPE accounts and to present
fairly the financial condition and performance of the LGU in the financial
statements.

3.7 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer to provide
the Municipal Accountant the status of on-going infrastructure projects as
basis of the latter 10in preparing the necessary journal entry vouchers to record
the partial/unbilled work accomplished to present the correct balances of the
affected PPE and payable accounts in the financial statements.

4.7 We recommended that the LCE:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal
Accountant and recommend to the LCE to request authority to write –
off the receivables in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular
No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023 and COA Circular 97-001,
whichever is applicable, and use the receiving copies of
demand/collection letters to support the request for authority to write-
off.
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor and analyze on a regular
basis all receivable accounts to ensure collection when they become due
and demandable. Also, determine the probability of collecting the
receivables and if collection is deemed unlikely, provide allowance for
impairment to ensure that the account is fairly presented in the
financial statements.

5.7 We recommended that the LCE:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the SSMI, original copy
of RIS, Certified List of Recipients to support the recorded issuances
of inventories to avoid suspension in audit.
b. Instruct the Supply/Property Officer to consolidate the weekly RIS of
supplies and materials using the SSMI and submit the same to the
Municipal Accountant as the basis in recording the issuances of
inventories to the end-users, to establish the reliability of Inventory
and related expense accounts.

6.5 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjustments to take up the
cancellation of the stale checks in their respective books of accounts to
reflect the correct balances of the cash in bank accounts and to restore the
corresponding amounts in the Accounts Payable account. Moreover, it is
recommended that at least one month before a check becomes stale, the
Municipal Treasurer send a written notice to the payee of the existence of
the check to facilitate the replacement thereof.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer to prepare the necessary documents detailing the information relative to replaced parts of road networks and furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant to serve as his basis in determining the appropriate carrying amount of replaced parts of local road networks to be derecognized from the books in order to present fairly the Road Networks account in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
1.4 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Inventory Committee to follow
the guidelines and procedures as provided for in COA Circular No. 2020-
006 dated January 31, 2020, in the conduct of physical count of PPE for the
one-time cleansing of PPE accounts of the Municipality to establish the
existence, condition and accuracy of PPE account balances. Moreover,
require them to derecognize all tangible items below the capitalization
threshold and follow the prescribed procedures in the disposition of
unserviceable properties in order not to overstate the PPE and related
accounts and to ascertain the fair presentation of the same in the financial
statements.

2.5 We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer to prepare the
necessary documents detailing the information relative to replaced parts of
road networks and furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant to serve as
his basis in determining the appropriate carrying amount of replaced parts
of local road networks to be derecognized from the books in order to present
fairly the Road Networks account in the financial statements.

3.10 We recommended that the LCE:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to send collection/demand letters (at least
three times) to the loan borrowers and ensure the same are duly received by
the latter. If no collection is made, coordinate with the Municipal Accountant
and recommend to the LCE to request authority to write –off the receivables in
accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August
17, 2023 and use the receiving copies of demand/collection letters to support
the request for authority to write-off.
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor and analyze on a regular basis
all receivable accounts to ensure collection when they become due and
demandable. Also, determine the probability of collecting the receivable and
if found that could no longer be collected, provide provision for impairment to
ensure that the account is fairly presented in the financial statements.

4.7 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Supply Officer to consolidate the
weekly RIS of supplies and materials and submit the same to the Municipal
Accountant as his basis in recording the issuances of inventories to the end-
users to establish the reliability of Inventory and related expense accounts.

5.4 We recommended that Management use the funds received in accordance
with the project proposals on a timely manner to maximize the beneficial use
of government projects. After which, effect the proper liquidation and remit
the remaining balance of the utilized funds to the source agency, hence in the
future, source agencies would be motivated to release more funds for other
projects.

6.4 We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to instruct the Secretariat to
call and hold a pre-procurement conference for procurement of all
infrastructure projects involving an ABC amounting to more than
P5,000,000.00 undertaken through public bidding as provided under Section
20 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to ensure readiness of the
procurement at hand.

7.8 We recommended that the LCE oblige the Municipal Engineer to monitor the
submission of the CHSP duly approved by the DOLE from the winning
bidders before the signing of contract to ensure the protection and welfare of
workers and the general public within and around the immediate vicinity of
the construction worksite. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to see to it
that payments of infrastructure projects are supported with valid Certificate of
CHSP approved by the DOLE, otherwise, deduct the corresponding cost of
implementing the same from the contractor’s claim to avoid disallowance in
audit. Moreover, require the Municipal Engineer to ensure that appropriate
safety measures are employed by the contractor so as to ensure protection and
welfare of workers and the general public from any untoward incident that may
occur during the implementation of the project.

8.7 We recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC Chairman to comply with the
documentary requirements and follow the prescribed rates in the computation
of honoraria based on the successfully completed projects and maximum rate
per procurement project to establish the validity of claims. Moreover, require
the Municipal Accountant to review the completeness of supporting documents
and correctness of said claims to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit.

9.4 We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to conduct the Pre-Bid
Conference within the prescribed period to allow the prospective bidders to
adequately prepare their bids.

10.7 We recommended that the LCE through the BAC to strictly adhere to the
pertinent provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of the RA No. 9184 on the
posting requirements of bid opportunities and notices to ensure transparency
and accountability in the procurement process.

11.3 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineering Office to
ensure that tarpaulin signboards are suitably framed for outdoor display to
inform the public on the status of the programs, projects and activities of the
municipality, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004. Moreover, require the
Municipal Accountant to see to it that payments of infrastructure projects are
supported with pictures of projects signboards that conform to the programmed
specification to avoid disallowance in audit.

12.4 We recommended that the LCE require the BAC, though its Secretariat,
see to it that the Notice of Award for the procurement of all infratrucrure
projects and goods through public bidding is posted in the PhilGEPS
website within three (3) calendar days from its issuance for information and
transparency in the procurement process.

13.4 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer to refrain
from using AF No. 56 to acknowledge the collections of taxes, licenses and
fees other than real property taxes in order not to defeat the purpose for which
the said accountable form has been authorized and to avoid improper
recording of collections in the books of accounts. Also, require her to monitor
the balances of accountable forms in their custody and determine the average
monthly usage of accountable forms as basis in the requisition thereof which
shall not exceed the two-month requirement of the Municipality.

14.6 We recommended that the LCE instruct the General Services Officer to assign
a knowledgeable personnel to check the details or review the propriety and
completeness of information on the trip tickets prior to official trips and to
regularly submit Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of
Fuel Consumption to the Auditor thru the Municipal Accountant for purposes
of verification on the reasonableness of fuel consumption. Moreover, require
the Municipal Accountant to review and verify the completeness of
documentary requirements for claims before affixing her signature on Box A
of each disbursement voucher to ascertain the validity and propriety of
disbursements made.

15.7 We recommended that the LCE instruct the BLPO to enhance their control
mechanism to validate the business owner’s or taxpayer’s declaration of gross
sales/receipts to ensure a reliable basis in the assessment of business taxes.
This includes the gathering of information from the Accounting Office on the
transactions made with business enterprises registered in the municipality to
determine the correctness of gross/sales receipts of business owners. Thus,
the BLPO could properly assess the tax, hence the LGU could collect more
revenues to finance its beneficial projects for its constituents. Moreover,
strictly impose the penalty of perjury to encourage business owners to declare
truthful information in the application form and to enable the BPLO assess the
rightful tax.

16.7 We recommended that the LCE enforce the collection of these taxes, and:
a. require the Municipal Treasurer to exert more efforts to collect from the
taxpayers and for those who have been unable to pay their taxes for several
years, issue notices of delinquency for them to settle their unpaid taxes;
b. if circumstances so warrant, avail of the Civil remedies provided for by
the provisions of the Local Government Code (RA No. 7160), specifically
Sections 174 to 176 thereof. Section 174 provides for the civil remedies
which are by (a) administrative action through distraint of personal
properties and (b) by judicial actions. Section 175 provides for the
procedure on the distraint of personal property and Section 176 provides
for the levy of Real Properties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the concerned municipal officials and employees employ effective strategies that would enhance the collections of long outstanding receivables so that the fund could also be used by the municipal government to finance other programs/projects.

Likewise, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts to the COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor supported with the required documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Not complied.
observations.reason: The uncollected livelihood loans is recorded under the account of Loans Receivable-Others.  As of December 31, 2018, the amount of P4,820,2774.87 still reflected in the financial statement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, the General Services Officer and the Municipal Engineer coordinate closely in the conduct of inventory and maintenance of appropriate records such as inventory report, road map, subsidiary ledger,  a record of a complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects and assessment report of the local road at every reporting date in compliance with COA Circular 2015-008  regarding the accounting and reporting on the Local Roads Asset Management System in consistent with the adoption of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) in the LGU.  Also, the Local Chief Executive should enjoin the department heads’ compliance with the requirements of the circular.

We recommended that the municipality settle its contractual obligation with the contractor to avoid any legal impediments.  Also, the liability and infrastructure (PPE) accounts should be recognized in the books of accounts.  Proper disclosure should be made in the financial statements for the receivable account on the transfer of the grant from the DA.

We recommended that management comply with the provisions of Sections 7.4 and 7.7 of COA Circular No. 94-006 in order to have timely inspection of goods procured and to determine the existence of deliveries subject for distribution and consumption of beneficiaries and end-users.

We recommended that the municipality submit the approved project proposals with pre-listing of beneficiaries and budgetary requirements; and properly accomplished lists of recipients as bases to evaluate the proper implementation of the projects/programs which were funded under RA 7171.

We recommended that management inform the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure projects or before the start of the program or activity that appropriate project signboards or public notices are already posted in compliance with Section 3.1 of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that traveling officials and employees properly prepare their itinerary of travels in accordance with the prescribed rates.  Moreover, a justifiable travel period  should only be granted to them.  Management should set a more reasonable guidelines on the allowable travel period and restriction on the   number of employees authorized to travel relative to the purpose of the travels in order to avoid unnecessary expenditures.

We recommended that the overpaid terminal leave benefits be refunded.  Also, the HRMO should comply with the provision of Section 25 (b) of the CSC Memorandum Circular 41, s. 1998 re: Amendments to the Omnibus Leave Rules (CSC Resolution No. 983142) for the forfeiture of 5-day mandatory vacation leave.  Also, earned leave credits should be accurately computed in order to avoid disallowance of transactions.

We recommended that the municipality insure other properties such as the municipal buildings, school buildings, hospitals and health centers, markets and other structures with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS in order to ensure that the LGU will be indemnified or compensated in case those properties will suffer any damage or loss due to fire, earthquake, storm, or other fortuitous event.
